elements.name: vaadin-chart-series
elements.description:
`<vaadin-chart-series>` is a custom element for creating series for Vaadin Charts.

### Basic use

To use `<vaadin-chart-series>`, add it inside a `<vaadin-chart>` element:

```html
<vaadin-chart>
  <vaadin-chart-series></vaadin-chart-series>
</vaadin-chart>
```

`<vaadin-chart-series>` accepts `values` as an array attribute, so you can add it to your element definition:

```html
<vaadin-chart-series values="[10, 20, 30, 40, 50]"></vaadin-chart-series>
```

which will add a new line series, where each value will be a data point.
Look for the Properties session to see all available attributes.

### Dynamically adding and removing series

You are also able to add and remove series by using DOM API.

To create a new series, call `document.createElement('vaadin-chart-series')` and append it to your `<vaadin-chart>`:

```js
const chart = document.querySelector('vaadin-chart');
const newSeries = document.createElement('vaadin-chart-series');
newSeries.values = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50];
chart.appendChild(newSeries);
```

In order to remove it, you should use the series to be removed as a reference for the `#removeChild()` call:

```js
const chart = document.querySelector('vaadin-chart');
const seriesToBeRemoved = chart.querySelector('vaadin-chart-series');
chart.removeChild(seriesToBeRemoved);
```

attributes.name: .additionalOptions
attributes.description: Represents additional JSON configuration.

attributes.name: .markers
attributes.description:
Shows/hides data-point markers for line-like series.
Acceptable input are:
 - `shown`: markers are always visible
 - `hidden`: markers are always hidden
 - `auto`: markers are visible for widespread data and hidden, when data is dense *(default)*

attributes.name: .neckPosition
attributes.description:
The height of the neck, the lower part of the funnel.
A number defines pixel width, a percentage string defines a percentage of the plot area height. Defaults to 30%.
Note that this property only applies for "funnel" charts.

attributes.name: .neckWidth
attributes.description:
The width of the neck, the lower part of the funnel.
A number defines pixel width, a percentage string defines a percentage of the plot area width. Defaults to 30%.
Note that this property only applies for "funnel" charts.

attributes.name: .stack
attributes.description:
Used to group series in a different stacks.
"stacking" property should be specified either for each series or in plotOptions.
It is recommended to place series in a single stack, when they belong to the same yAxis.

attributes.name: .title
attributes.description: The name of the series as shown in the legend, tooltip etc.

attributes.name: .type
attributes.description:
A string with the type of the series.
Defaults to `'line'` in case no type is set for the chart.
Note that `'bar'`, `'gauge'` and `'solidgauge'` should be set as default series type on `<vaadin-chart>`.

attributes.name: .unit
attributes.description:
Used to connect the series to an axis; if multiple series have the same `unit`, they will share axis.
Displayed as a title for the axis.
If no unit is defined, then series will be connected to the first axis.

attributes.name: .valueMax
attributes.description:
Value-axis maximum-value.
See the 'valueMin'

attributes.name: .valueMin
attributes.description:
Value-axis minimum-value.
Sets the value to a series bound by 'unit' property.
Otherwise sets the value to the first series.
Undefined by default (determined from data).

attributes.name: .values
attributes.description:
An array of data used by the series.
Format depends on the chart type and can be:
  - An array of numerical values `[y0, y1, y2, y3,...]`
  - An array of arrays with 2 values (`x`, `y`) `[ [x0, y0], [x1, y1], [x2, y2], ... ]`
  - An array of objects, each one describing one point `[ {x: x0, y: y0, name: 'Point0', color: '#FF0000'}, {...}, ...]`

 See more in [API Site](https://api.highcharts.com/highcharts/series)

elements.name: vaadin-chart

attributes.name: ?chart3d
attributes.description:
Specifies whether to show chart in 3 or in 2 dimensions.
Some display angles are added by default to the "chart.options3d" (`{alpha: 15, beta: 15, depth: 50}`).
3D display options can be modified via `additionalOptions`.
The thickness of a Pie chart can be set on `additionalOptions` through `plotOptions.pie.depth`.
3D is supported by Bar, Column, Pie and Scatter3D charts.
More info available at [Highcharts](https://www.highcharts.com/docs/chart-concepts/3d-charts).

attributes.name: ?noLegend
attributes.description:
Specifies whether to hide legend or show.
Legend configuration can be set up via additionalOptions property

attributes.name: ?polar
attributes.description:
When present, cartesian charts like line, spline, area and column are transformed
into the polar coordinate system.

attributes.name: ?timeline
attributes.description:
Specifies whether the chart is a normal chart or a timeline chart.
Value of this property is ignored for Gantt charts (type="gantt").

attributes.name: ?tooltip
attributes.description: Whether or not to show tooltip when hovering data points.

attributes.name: .additionalOptions
attributes.description: Represents additional JSON configuration.

attributes.name: .categories
attributes.description:
If categories are present names are used instead of numbers for the category axis.
The format of categories can be an `Array` with a list of categories, such as `['2010', '2011', '2012']`
or a mapping `Object`, like `{0:'1',9:'Target (10)', 15: 'Max'}`.

attributes.name: .categoryMax
attributes.description: Category-axis maximum value. Defaults to `undefined`.

attributes.name: .categoryMin
attributes.description: Category-axis minimum value. Defaults to `undefined`.

attributes.name: .categoryPosition
attributes.description:
The position of the category axis. Acceptable values are `left`, `right`, `top` and `bottom`
except for bar charts which only accept `left` and `right`.
With the default value, charts appear as though they have `category-position="bottom"`
except for bar charts that appear as though they have `category-position="left"`.

Defaults to `undefined`

attributes.name: .configuration
attributes.description:
Configuration object that exposes the JS Api to configure the chart.

Most important methods are:
- `addSeries (Object options, [Boolean redraw], [Mixed animation])`
- `addAxis (Object options, [Boolean isX], [Boolean redraw], [Mixed animation])`
- `setTitle (Object title, object subtitle, Boolean redraw)`

Most important properties are:
- `configuration.series`: An array of the chart's series. Detailed API for Series object is
    available in [API Site](http://api.highcharts.com/class-reference/Highcharts.Series)
- `configuration.xAxis`: An array of the chart's x axes. Detailed API for Axis object is
    available in [API Site](http://api.highcharts.com/class-reference/Highcharts.Axis)
- `configuration.yAxis`: An array of the chart's y axes. Detailed API for Axis object is
    available in [API Site](http://api.highcharts.com/class-reference/Highcharts.Axis)
- `configuration.title`: The chart title.

For detailed documentation of available API check the [API site](http://api.highcharts.com/class-reference/classes.list)

attributes.name: .emptyText
attributes.description: Specifies the message displayed on a chart without displayable data.

attributes.name: .stacking
attributes.description:
Specifies how series are stacked on top of each other.
Possible values are null, "normal" or "percent".
If "stack" property is not defined on the vaadin-chart-series elements, then series will be put into
the default stack.

attributes.name: .subtitle
attributes.description: Represents the subtitle of the chart.

attributes.name: .title
attributes.description: Represents the title of the chart.

attributes.name: .type
attributes.description:
Sets the default series type of the chart.
Note that `'bar'`, `'gauge'` and `'solidgauge'` should be set as default series type.

attributes.name: @chart-add-series
attributes.description: Fired when a new series is added.

attributes.name: @chart-after-export
attributes.description: Fired after a chart is exported.

attributes.name: @chart-after-print
attributes.description: Fired after a chart is printed.

attributes.name: @chart-before-export
attributes.description: Fired before a chart is exported.

attributes.name: @chart-before-print
attributes.description: Fired before a chart is printed.

attributes.name: @chart-click
attributes.description: Fired when clicking on the plot background.

attributes.name: @chart-drilldown
attributes.description: Fired when drilldown point is clicked.

attributes.name: @chart-drillup
attributes.description: Fired when drilling up from a drilldown series.

attributes.name: @chart-drillupall
attributes.description: Fired after all the series has been drilled up if chart has multiple drilldown series.

attributes.name: @chart-end-resize
attributes.description: Fired when the chart finishes resizing.

attributes.name: @chart-load
attributes.description: Fired when the chart is finished loading.

attributes.name: @chart-redraw
attributes.description:
Fired when the chart is redraw. Can be called after a `Chart.configuration.redraw()`
or after an axis, series or point is modified with the `redraw` option set to `true`.

attributes.name: @chart-selection
attributes.description: Fired when an area of the chart has been selected.

attributes.name: @point-click
attributes.description: Fired when the point is clicked.

attributes.name: @point-drag
attributes.description: Fired while dragging a point.

attributes.name: @point-drag-start
attributes.description: Fired when starting to drag a point.

attributes.name: @point-drop
attributes.description: Fired when the point is dropped.

attributes.name: @point-legend-item-click
attributes.description: Fired when the legend item belonging to the point is clicked.

attributes.name: @point-mouse-out
attributes.description: Fired when the mouse leaves the area close to the point.

attributes.name: @point-mouse-over
attributes.description: Fired when the mouse enters the area close to the point.

attributes.name: @point-remove
attributes.description: Fired when the point is removed from the series.

attributes.name: @point-select
attributes.description: Fired when the point is selected either programmatically or by clicking on the point.

attributes.name: @point-unselect
attributes.description: Fired when the point is unselected either programmatically or by clicking on the point

attributes.name: @point-update
attributes.description: Fired when the point is updated programmatically through `.updateConfiguration()` method.

attributes.name: @series-after-animate
attributes.description: Fired when the series has finished its initial animation.

attributes.name: @series-checkbox-click
attributes.description: Fired when the checkbox next to the series' name in the legend is clicked.

attributes.name: @series-click
attributes.description: Fired when the series is clicked.

attributes.name: @series-hide
attributes.description: Fired when the series is hidden after chart generation time.

attributes.name: @series-legend-item-click
attributes.description: Fired when the legend item belonging to the series is clicked.

attributes.name: @series-mouse-out
attributes.description: Fired when the mouses leave the graph.

attributes.name: @series-mouse-over
attributes.description: Fired when the mouse enters the graph.

attributes.name: @series-show
attributes.description: Fired when the series is show after chart generation time.

attributes.name: @xaxes-extremes-set
attributes.description: Fired when the minimum and maximum is set for the x axis.

attributes.name: @yaxes-extremes-set
attributes.description: Fired when the minimum and maximum is set for the y axis.
